title: On some dyadic models of the Euler equations

sec:1.summary: Reviews inviscid Burgers blowup via method of characteristics. Shows $H^\alpha$ blowup for $\alpha \ge 1/2 (generic)$ or $\alpha \ge 5/6 (non-generic)$. Frames the open incompressible Euler singularity question and states the paper's goal: KP model = dyadic Burgers, blowup for all $\alpha > 0$.

sec:2.summary: Derives the dyadic inviscid Burgers equation (eq:12) by Fourier-transforming conservative Burgers, restricting to odd solutions with dyadic wavenumbers k = 2^j. The chain ODE $da_j/dt = 2^j(a_{j-1}^2 - 2a_j a_{j+1})$ emerges from the quadratic self-interaction after artificial dyadic restriction.

sec:3.summary: Proves KP wavelet model (eq:13) reduces exactly to dyadic Burgers (eq:12) for initial data uniform across same-level cubes. The substitution $a_j = 2^{3j/2} u_j$ transforms KP chain (eq:14-15) into dyadic Burgers up to time rescaling. The 2^3 factor from 8 children per cube absorbs into the variable change.

sec:4.summary: Analyzes the Friedlander-Pavlovic model (eq:18). Shows it is structurally similar to dyadic Burgers but with $\lambda = 2^{5/2}$ instead of $\lambda = 2$. Identifies inconsistencies in FP's derivation: symmetrization of the gradient matrix $B^T$ is unjustified, and the coupling coefficient 2^{5j/2} overestimates single-Fourier-mode interactions.

lem:1.summary: Energy transfer lemma for the general chain model (eq:19). If tail energy $E_{B(j)}(t_0) \ge q^j$ for $\lambda^{-2} <$ q < 1, then within time $\rho^j$ there exists t such that $E_{B(j+1)}(t) \ge q^{j+1}$. Proof by contradiction: assuming the shifted tail stays small forces $a_j$ to be large (from energy gap), which drives $a_{j+1}$ growth via the nonlinear coupling, contradicting the upper bound.

thm:1.summary: MAIN RESULT: For the general chain model (eq:19) with $\lambda > 1$, non-negative initial data, and $E_{B(J)}(0) \ge q^J$, the $H^\alpha$ norm blows up in finite time for ANY $\alpha > 0$. Proof iterates Lemma 1: energy cascades to wavenumber J+k at time $t_k$, giving ‖a‖$^2_{H^\alpha} \ge \mu^{2\alpha(J+k)} q^{J+k} \to \infty$ provided $\mu^{2\alpha}q > 1$. Blowup time is finite: $t_k \le \rho^J/(1-\rho)$. Applies to KP $(\lambda=2)$, FP $(\lambda=2^{5/2})$, and all intermediate models.

sec:6.summary: Discusses Obukhov's 1971 shell model (eq:40) as an alternative: instability cascade rather than nonlinear steepening. Notes the Kolmogorov-Obukhov $k^{-5/3}$ power-law steady state (eq:41) is shared by both model classes. Poses open question: what blowup occurs in the inviscid Obukhov model and is there a connection to the power law?

edges.note: KP chain (eq:14-15) is shown identical to dyadic Burgers (eq:12) via $a_j = 2^{3j/2} u_j$. The derivation in sec:2 provides the target form.
FP model (eq:18) is structurally compared to dyadic Burgers. Same chain form but with $\lambda = 2^{5/2}$ vs $\lambda = 2$.
FP model analysis references the KP reduction to contextualize the overestimated coupling coefficients.
Uses the general chain model (eq:19) which generalizes dyadic Burgers, plus its forward energy flux property (eq:22) and nonnegativity preservation.
Proof iterates Lemma 1 k times to cascade energy from wavenumber J to J+k. The geometric time sum $t_k \le \rho^J/(1-\rho)$ gives finite blowup time.
Applies blowup to KP model: since KP = dyadic Burgers $(\lambda=2)$, Theorem 1 gives $H^\alpha$ blowup for all $\alpha > 0$, strengthening KP04's $H^{3/2+\varepsilon}$ result.
Applies blowup to FP model $(\lambda=2^{5/2})$: any r > 0 suffices, vs FP04's restriction r > 3/2.

model_derivation.description: Derives the dyadic inviscid Burgers equation from Fourier-restricted 1D Burgers and reviews classical blowup

model_reduction.description: Shows KP and FP models reduce to or belong to the dyadic Burgers class of chain models

blowup_proof.description: Proves finite-time $H^\alpha$ blowup for any $\alpha > 0$ for the entire class of chain models with $\lambda > 1$

extensions.description: Comparison with Obukhov cascade model and open questions about blowup-power law connection

KP04.title: Finite time blow-up for a dyadic model of the Euler equations

FP04.title: Blow-up in a three-dimensional vector model for the Euler equations

DS01.title: A quasilinear approximation for the three-dimensional Navier-Stokes system

Wds04.title: Remarks on a quasilinear model of the Navier-Stokes equations
